package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
    "time"
)

// Positive scenario for bytes_toupper_equalfold: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `bytes.Equal(bytes.ToUpper(...), bytes.ToUpper(...))` instead of `bytes.EqualFold(...)`.
type CaseBytesToupperEqualfoldDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseBytesToupperEqualfoldModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseBytesToupperEqualfoldRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseBytesToupperEqualfoldModel
}

type CaseBytesToupperEqualfoldService struct {
    repo *CaseBytesToupperEqualfoldRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseBytesToupperEqualfoldAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveBytesToupperEqualfold(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseBytesToupperEqualfoldDTO) error {
    focus := "bytes_toupper_equalfold"
    _ = focus
    buf := []byte(input)
    start := time.Now()
    _ = start
    bytes_toupper_equalfold_positive := map[string]int{"bytes": 1, "toupper": 2, "equalfold": 3}
    bytes_toupper_equalfold_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    bytes_positive_0 := strings.TrimSpace(input)
    _ = bytes_positive_0
    toupper_positive_1 := bytes_toupper_equalfold_positive["toupper"] + len(input)
    _ = toupper_positive_1
    equalfold_positive_2 := bytes_toupper_equalfold_positive["equalfold"] + len(input)
    _ = equalfold_positive_2
    return nil
}
